summary: Directory-scoped LB slice split from a larger matching-improvement branch. The PR made small expression-ordering, temporary-variable, stack-padding, and arithmetic rewrites across src/melee/lb to improve decompilation matching/reviewability without introducing reported regressions. Human review feedback was minimal but positive: PsiLupan commented that the changes LGTM.
what_changed: In lb_8000D148, reordered local declarations and changed squared-distance construction to square diff_02_x/diff_12_x in place before summing with squared y components.
In lb_80011E24, removed the redundant out alias for result, wrote directly through result, reordered locals, and added PAD_STACK(4).
In lb_80012994, rewrote blur-size scaling from multiplication by 0.015625f to division by 64.0f.
In lbBgFlash_80020E38, inlined the temporary z variable into HSD_JObjSetRotationZ(jobj, angle + HSD_JObjGetRotationZ(jobj)).
In lbColl_800077A0, moved dot_diff_cb calculation after diff_ba component assignment.
In lbDvd_80018254, removed an unused struct lbDvd_803B72C0_t* var_r26 local while retaining existing PAD_STACK(0x18).

In lbRefract_80021CE8, reordered y_tex declaration, folded dist_sq assignment into the if condition, and introduced abs_dist/abs_param0 temporaries around fabsf comparisons before the remainder calculation.
The PR was explicitly scoped to src/melee/lb as a split from #2617, intended to make a larger set of matching improvements easier to review.
smart_moves: Kept the PR small and directory-scoped, which reduced reviewer burden for a larger unsplit matching branch.
Used source-preserving expression reshapes rather than semantic rewrites: temporary removal, assignment folding, declaration ordering, and equivalent arithmetic forms.
Added explicit PAD_STACK(4) in lb_80011E24 instead of relying on an unrelated local alias to influence stack/codegen shape.
Preferred direct result pointer mutation over an extra HSD_JObj** alias when the alias was only serving matching/codegen needs.
Introduced local abs_dist and abs_param0 temporaries in lbRefract_80021CE8, likely to better match compiler scheduling/register allocation while also clarifying the comparison.
Removed an unused local in lbDvd_80018254, tightening the decompiled C while preserving the existing stack pad.
decomp_lessons: For matching work, declaration order and whether a temporary exists can materially affect generated code even when C semantics are unchanged.
Equivalent floating-point constants can produce different codegen; try both multiplication by a reciprocal and division by the literal denominator when chasing a match.
Assignment inside a conditional, as in if ((dist_sq = x * x + y_sq) > 0.0f), can be a useful tactic when matching original evaluation order.
When removing an alias or unused local changes stack layout, explicit PAD_STACK can preserve the required frame shape more honestly.
Small in-place squaring transformations, such as diff_x *= diff_x followed by sum construction, can better reflect original instruction ordering than assigning dist = diff_x * diff_x first.
Split large matching branches by directory/subsystem when possible; this PR body shows that isolated path slices with diff-check results are easier to review and merge.
Do not infer semantic intent from these changes beyond matching/codegen cleanup; the PR context frames the work as matching improvements and gives no evidence of gameplay or algorithm changes.
naming_conventions: LB library functions use file/subsystem-prefixed names such as lb_8000D148, lb_80011E24, lb_80012994, lbBgFlash_80020E38, lbColl_800077A0, lbDvd_80018254, and lbRefract_80021CE8.
Temporary geometry variables are named descriptively by point pair and axis, such as diff_02_x, diff_02_y, diff_12_x, diff_12_y, dist_squared_02, and dist_squared_12.
Matrix temporaries use CamelCase-like names matching HSD conventions, such as quatMtx and rotMtx.
Callback and data types retain subsystem prefixes, such as lbRefract_CallbackData, PreloadEntry, and lbDvd_803B72C0_t.
Stack-padding macros are written as PAD_STACK(byte_count), seen here as PAD_STACK(4) and PAD_STACK(0x18).
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Reorder local declarations to influence register allocation and stack layout.
Remove redundant aliases when direct pointer mutation gives better matching codegen.
Use PAD_STACK to compensate for stack-frame shape after local-variable cleanup.
Inline single-use temporaries when the original code likely computed directly into a call argument.
Move independent calculations across nearby assignments to match instruction scheduling.
Fold an assignment into an if condition to constrain evaluation order.
Introduce small nested scopes and explicit abs_* temporaries around __fabsf calls to influence lifetime and register reuse.
Try division by a power-of-two float literal, such as / 64.0f, instead of multiplication by its decimal reciprocal when matching floating-point code.

review_feedback.author: PsiLupan
review_feedback.impact: Approved the scoped LB matching changes without requesting further revisions.

follow_up_queries: Find other PRs split from #2617 for subsystem-specific matching tactics.
Search for PAD_STACK additions paired with removal of unused locals or pointer aliases.
Search for cases where reciprocal multiplication was changed to division by a float literal for matching.
Compare lbRefract_80021CE8 matching history around __frsqrte and __fabsf codegen.
Review future lb_80011E24 changes for result pointer aliasing and stack-layout sensitivity.
